title: Shadows over Phandalin
subtitle: Family-friendly starter, prepared sessions and a living town
description: Frontier sample campaign designed to teach session prep, clear clues, memorable NPCs, town consequences and the player portal.
summary: Phandalin is a frontier town built over old ruins, unsafe roads and mining promises. Gundren’s disappearance pushes the party toward goblins, bandits, frightened neighbors and a threat still operating from afar.
recommendedFor: DMs who want to try DMCC with an easy-to-run table campaign, ideal for families or new groups: prepared scenes, clear goals, public material for the player portal and enough secrets to practice revelations without overwhelming the table.
pitch: A starter campaign for learning DMCC through a comfortable structure: central town, clear quests, memorable NPCs, visible clues and consequences that make players feel Phandalin changing with their decisions.
learningGoals: Prepare classic adventure sessions with goals, clues and a short checklist.
Use the town as a living hub where each NPC can react to the party’s actions.
Separate public clues, DM secrets and consequences that change Phandalin’s tone.
Publish safe player-facing information without revealing the whole mystery.
includedMaterial: Five prepared sessions from the opening ambush to the lost mine.
Town NPCs, factions, locations, clues, rumors and table handouts.
Consequence arcs for freeing Phandalin or letting fear grow.
A threat canvas for reading Redbrands, Gundren and the Black Spider at a glance.

quickStart.title: How to start Shadows over Phandalin
steps: Open the fronts canvas and find three axes: missing Gundren, Redbrands in town and the mine as a future promise.
Run session 1 as a simple adventure: ambush, clear clue and a choice to follow tracks or protect supplies.
In session 2, use town NPCs so every player has someone to talk to.
When the party acts against the Redbrands, record how Phandalin’s mood changes as a consequence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_note.title: How to use Phandalin in DMCC
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.summary: This example teaches DMCC’s most direct flow: prepare a session, run clear scenes, record clues and let the town react to the party.
ent_tpl_phandalin_note.content: This template is designed for family tables: clear goals, recognizable NPCs and simple decisions with visible consequences.

ent_tpl_phandalin_town.title: Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.subtitle: Frontier town
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.summary: A small settlement trying to prosper while living under external threats and internal intimidation.
ent_tpl_phandalin_town.content: Phandalin was resettled a generation ago over the bones of an older town — cellars, wells and cracked foundations still surface when someone digs too deep. Most families arrived chasing cheap land near the old dwarven road; what they found instead was a town too small to police itself and too stubborn to leave.

ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.title: Triboar Trail
ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.summary: The road where the party finds the first signs that Gundren has been attacked.
ent_tpl_phandalin_trail.content: Triboar Trail carries most of the traffic between Phandalin and the Sword Coast, which makes it worth watching. Goblins from Cragmaw have grown bold enough to strike within sight of the crossroads, banking on the fact that nobody in Phandalin has the manpower to chase them into the woods.

ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.title: Cragmaw Hideout
ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.summary: A goblin cave that teaches the group Gundren’s disappearance was not random.
ent_tpl_phandalin_hideout.content: A shallow cave system with a stream running through it, chosen by the Cragmaw because the water muffles footsteps and drowns torchlight before it can warn anyone deeper in. Klarg holds it through fear and stolen loot, not loyalty — exactly the crack the party can use.

ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.title: Tresendar Manor
ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.summary: Ruins hiding the Redbrand base and several moral choices for the group.
ent_tpl_phandalin_tresendar.content: Once the seat of a minor noble family, Tresendar Manor burned decades ago and was never rebuilt above ground. Below it, the old cellars survived intact: cool, dry and forgotten by everyone except the people using them as a base now.

ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.title: Wave Echo Cave
ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.summary: Final destination of Gundren’s search and the heart of the conflict over the Forge of Spells.
ent_tpl_phandalin_wave_echo.content: Wave Echo Cave was sealed after an earlier expedition ended in disaster, and the stories that leaked out afterward kept most treasure hunters away for a generation. What's left inside is worth more than gold: a working forge older than Phandalin itself.

ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.title: Gundren Rockseeker
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.subtitle: Dwarven prospector
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.summary: He hired the party to escort supplies. His map is the piece everyone wants.
ent_tpl_phandalin_gundren.content: Gundren talks fast and trusts easily, which is either his best quality or the reason his brothers keep bailing him out. He fronted the coin for this expedition on the strength of a map he wouldn't fully explain, even to Sildar.

ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.title: Sildar Hallwinter
ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.subtitle: Veteran and potential ally
ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.summary: He can explain the context and give the group a reason to protect Phandalin.
ent_tpl_phandalin_sildar.content: Sildar served with the Lords' Alliance long enough to know when a job has gone sideways. He came to Phandalin as Gundren's second, not as a hero, and he'd rather talk his way out of danger than fight — though he won't hesitate if the party needs backup.

ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.title: Redbrands
ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.subtitle: Red-cloaked thugs
ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.summary: They control the town’s fear through low-intensity violence and public threats.
ent_tpl_phandalin_redbrands.content: The Redbrands showed up in Phandalin about a year ago, small-time enough that nobody took them seriously until the first shop burned. They don't need every resident afraid of them — just enough of them quiet.

ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.title: Iarno Albrek / Glasstaff
ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.subtitle: Missing wizard with a double identity
ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.summary: Sildar’s clue points to a lost ally, but the truth connects him to the Redbrands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_iarno.content: Iarno arrived in Phandalin claiming to be a scholar interested in old ruins, and for a while people believed him. Whatever scruples he had didn't survive contact with easy money and the promise of standing over Nezznar's operation instead of under it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_nezznar.title: Nezznar, the Black Spider
ent_tpl_phandalin_nezznar.summary: He wants to reach Wave Echo Cave first and control the Forge.
ent_tpl_phandalin_nezznar.content: Nezznar was driven out of the Underdark generations before the current war began, and Wave Echo Cave has been his obsession ever since — not for the gold, but for what the forge represents to a creature who was never meant to see daylight again.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_gundren.title: Find Gundren
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_gundren.summary: The main thread: follow the trail from the ambush to Cragmaw and eventually the mine.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_gundren.content: This thread should never go cold for more than a scene. Every location the party visits should offer one more thread pointing toward the mine, even before they understand what they're chasing.

metadata.objective: rescue Gundren and recover the map

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.title: Dead horses on the road
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.summary: The first physical clue: the attack was prepared and Gundren was ahead of the party.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dead_horses.content: The horses were killed quietly — no panic, no bolting, just two bodies dropped exactly where they'd block the road. Whoever set this ambush wanted the wagon stopped, not destroyed.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.title: Iarno is Glasstaff
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.summary: Sildar’s missing ally leads or manipulates the Redbrands from Tresendar.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_iarno.content: Iarno kept the wizard persona alive because it let him move through town without suspicion, buying supplies and asking questions no one thought twice about. The moment that mask slips, every favor he's called in becomes a liability.

ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.title: Phandalin finds its voice again
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.summary: If the Redbrands fall, the town starts acting, speaking and asking for help in visible ways.
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_town.content: Confidence returns slowly and unevenly — not everyone speaks up at once. Give a couple of scenes to specific NPCs finding their nerve before treating the whole town as safe.

ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.title: Stonehill Inn
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.subtitle: Safe place for conversations
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.summary: Social hub where the party can hear rumors, rest and measure the town’s fear.
ent_tpl_phandalin_inn.content: Use it to introduce NPCs without forcing scenes: someone is always listening, exaggerating or asking for help.

ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.title: Shrine of Luck
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.subtitle: Small refuge with useful information
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.summary: A calm place where a kind conversation can provide clues about disappearances and night movements.
ent_tpl_phandalin_shrine.content: Good place for players seeking help without going through violence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.title: Toblen Stonehill
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.subtitle: Innkeeper who knows more than he wants to admit
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.summary: He hears everyone’s rumors, but fears the Redbrands will punish his family.
ent_tpl_phandalin_toblen.content: Toblen helps if the group shows they are not here just to provoke a fight.

ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.title: Linene Graywind
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.subtitle: Pragmatic merchant
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.summary: She wants safe roads and does not trust improvised heroes until she sees results.
ent_tpl_phandalin_linene.content: She can offer gear, information about lost supplies or a dry warning about the Redbrands.

ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.title: Halia Thornton
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.subtitle: Ambitious, useful and dangerous
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.summary: She may help against the Redbrands if that increases her influence over Phandalin.
ent_tpl_phandalin_halia.content: Halia gives practical information, but always tries to keep a political advantage.

ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.title: Qelline Alderleaf
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.subtitle: Kind neighbor with discreet contacts
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.summary: She knows less-watched roads and can point the group toward outside help.
ent_tpl_phandalin_qelline.content: Ideal for family tables: offers warmth, food and a nonviolent way to gain direction.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.title: Break the Redbrands’ fear
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.subtitle: Urban arc of Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.summary: The group must move from hearing complaints to giving the town a concrete reason to act.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_break_fear.content: It is not solved only by defeating enemies: the town changes when it sees protection, proof or leadership.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.title: Note with a black seal
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.subtitle: Clue toward a distant hand
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.summary: A short note orders Gundren kept alive until the full map is found.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_blackseal.content: It does not reveal everything about Nezznar, but introduces that the Redbrands are not the end of the chain.

metadata.content: The order comes from someone signing with a black spider, not from Glasstaff.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.title: Boots with red clay
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.subtitle: Physical clue in town
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.summary: Several bandit boots carry clay from the old path toward Tresendar.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redclay.content: Lets the group find the base without relying on interrogations.

metadata.content: The clay matches the abandoned path below Tresendar Manor.

ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.title: The old well sings at night
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.subtitle: Rumor to color the town
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.summary: Children and miners say the well returns echoes that belong to no one in town.
ent_tpl_phandalin_rumor_well.content: It can be local color or a soft clue toward old ruins under Phandalin.

ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.title: Gundren’s torn map
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.subtitle: Player handout
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.summary: Map fragment with an incomplete mark toward Wave Echo Cave.
ent_tpl_phandalin_handout_map.content: Give only one part at first. The second part can appear in Tresendar or with Gundren.

ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.title: Fight on the main street
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.subtitle: Flexible encounter against thugs
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.summary: The Redbrands test whether the group can be intimidated in front of frightened neighbors.
ent_tpl_phandalin_encounter_street.content: Can be resolved through combat, public humiliation, hard negotiation or a social trap.

ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.title: The town starts organizing
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.subtitle: Positive consequence
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.summary: If the group protects specific neighbors, Phandalin stops waiting for saviors and starts acting.
ent_tpl_phandalin_consequence_people_ris.content: Trigger this consequence when players help a named person, not just when they win a fight.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.title: The Black Spider funds chaos from afar
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.subtitle: Campaign secret
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.summary: Nezznar does not need to control Phandalin directly; he only needs nobody looking toward the mine.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar_funds.content: Lets the Redbrands be a local problem connected to a larger threat without turning everything into exposition.

metadata.truth: Nezznar uses intermediaries to keep the town distracted while he searches for Wave Echo Cave.

ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_aric.title: Aric Veldan
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_aric.subtitle: Human fighter
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_aric.summary: Human warrior and caravan escort. Wants to prove he is worth more than a hired sword.
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_aric.content: Aric grew up watching caravan guards get paid more respect than soldiers, and he's spent every job since trying to close that gap. He follows orders well but chafes when nobody explains the plan.

ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_mira.title: Mira Nightrill
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_mira.subtitle: Halfling rogue
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_mira.summary: Halfling rogue, quick hands and even quicker to smell trouble.
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_mira.content: Mira learned to read a room before she learned to read words, and both skills still serve her. She left her old crew behind for reasons she won't discuss, though the scars on her knuckles hint at how the split went.

ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_brom.title: Brom Cindermug
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_brom.subtitle: Dwarf cleric
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_brom.summary: Dwarf cleric with rough hands and practical faith. Looking for a place where his help matters.
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_brom.content: Brom apprenticed as a smith before his god called him elsewhere, and his healing still smells faintly of the forge. He treats every wound like a repair job: patch it, test it, make sure it holds.

ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_nim.title: Nimue Ashbottle
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_nim.subtitle: Elf wizard
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_nim.summary: Curious and methodical elf wizard. Suspects Phandalin hides magic older than the current town.
ent_tpl_phandalin_pc_nim.content: Nimue left her family's library after finding a reference to Phandalin in a book that shouldn't have mentioned it at all. She hasn't told the party why yet, mostly because she isn't sure herself.

ent_tpl_phandalin_barthen.title: Barthen's Provisions
ent_tpl_phandalin_barthen.subtitle: Supply store
ent_tpl_phandalin_barthen.summary: Caravan destination and first friendly stop in town. Key for confirming Gundren's absence.
ent_tpl_phandalin_barthen.content: Elmar runs the biggest general store in town, and everything that arrives by caravan passes through his ledger first. If a shipment goes missing, he's the first to notice and the last to stay quiet about it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_lionshield.title: Lionshield Coster
ent_tpl_phandalin_lionshield.subtitle: Supply depot
ent_tpl_phandalin_lionshield.summary: Trading post with goblin-stolen cargo. Connects goblins to local commercial life.
ent_tpl_phandalin_lionshield.content: Lionshield Coster used to be Phandalin's most reliable trade post before goblins started raiding its shipments. Empty shelves and a nervous staff tell that story faster than anyone working there will.

ent_tpl_phandalin_sleeping_giant.title: Sleeping Giant
ent_tpl_phandalin_sleeping_giant.subtitle: Redbrand-held tavern
ent_tpl_phandalin_sleeping_giant.summary: Rough tavern where the thugs flaunt power, drink and test the group's authority.
ent_tpl_phandalin_sleeping_giant.content: The Sleeping Giant serves bad ale to worse company, and everyone in town knows to avoid it after dark. Redbrands drink loud enough that a careful listener can piece together more than the gang would like.

ent_tpl_phandalin_townmaster.title: Town Master's Hall
ent_tpl_phandalin_townmaster.subtitle: Harbin Wester's office
ent_tpl_phandalin_townmaster.summary: Official building with a notice board, minor contracts and the mayor's uncomfortable silences.
ent_tpl_phandalin_townmaster.content: Harbin's office is quieter than a town under siege has any right to be. The notice board says a lot about what he's willing to admit is a problem, and a lot more about what he isn't.

ent_tpl_phandalin_edermath.title: Edermath Orchard
ent_tpl_phandalin_edermath.subtitle: Veteran's retreat
ent_tpl_phandalin_edermath.summary: Quiet refuge for Daran, who observes the Redbrands with an old soldier's concern.
ent_tpl_phandalin_edermath.content: Daran keeps the orchard the way he kept formation decades ago: methodical, watchful, ready to move. He notices things soldiers notice — patrols, patterns, who's missing from town that shouldn't be.

ent_tpl_phandalin_alderleaf.title: Alderleaf Farm
ent_tpl_phandalin_alderleaf.subtitle: Qelline and Carp's home
ent_tpl_phandalin_alderleaf.summary: Warm family home reminding us Phandalin has families, not just quest hooks.
ent_tpl_phandalin_alderleaf.content: The farm smells like bread and tilled earth, and Carp treats every stranger who visits like a personal audience. Qelline lets him, mostly because a curious kid asking questions draws less suspicion than an adult doing the same.

ent_tpl_phandalin_miners_exchange.title: Miner's Exchange
ent_tpl_phandalin_miners_exchange.subtitle: Economic hub
ent_tpl_phandalin_miners_exchange.summary: Where finds are weighed and Halia measures who could govern the town's economic future.
ent_tpl_phandalin_miners_exchange.content: Every claim, rumor and grudge in Phandalin eventually gets weighed here, sometimes literally. Halia built her influence one favor at a time, and this is where she calls most of them in.

ent_tpl_phandalin_cragmaw_castle.title: Cragmaw Castle
ent_tpl_phandalin_cragmaw_castle.subtitle: Hidden fortress
ent_tpl_phandalin_cragmaw_castle.summary: Goblin-held ruin where Gundren, the map or both may change hands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_cragmaw_castle.content: The castle predates the goblins occupying it by centuries, its halls built for defenders who never returned. Grol rules from a throne room that was never meant for him, which he compensates for loudly.

ent_tpl_phandalin_forge_of_spells.title: Forge of Spells
ent_tpl_phandalin_forge_of_spells.subtitle: Arcane heart of the mine
ent_tpl_phandalin_forge_of_spells.summary: Damaged magical focus that can still charge weapons, awaken guardians and attract power-seekers.
ent_tpl_phandalin_forge_of_spells.content: The forge hasn't gone cold in longer than anyone alive can confirm, kept alight by whatever magic first shaped it. Getting close enough to use it means getting past everything that still wants to protect it, or possess it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_conyberry.title: Conyberry Ruins
ent_tpl_phandalin_conyberry.subtitle: Path to Agatha
ent_tpl_phandalin_conyberry.summary: Ruins of a destroyed village preparing a tone shift before entering ancient territory.
ent_tpl_phandalin_conyberry.content: Conyberry burned so long ago that even Agatha barely remembers what it looked like whole. What's left is scorched foundations, a few standing walls and the sense that something is still listening.

ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha_lair.title: Agatha's Lair
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha_lair.subtitle: Spectral refuge
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha_lair.summary: Place of supernatural negotiation where courtesy matters as much as magic.
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha_lair.content: Agatha's grave sits at the center of what used to be a village square, and she still expects visitors to observe the courtesies of the living. Rudeness gets you silence; a fair question, sometimes, gets you an answer.

ent_tpl_phandalin_old_owl_well.title: Old Owl Well
ent_tpl_phandalin_old_owl_well.subtitle: Arcane ruin
ent_tpl_phandalin_old_owl_well.summary: Ancient ruins with undead activity and a mage who does not quite fit as a central villain.
ent_tpl_phandalin_old_owl_well.content: The well marks older magic than anyone in Phandalin remembers building. Hamun Kost found that out the hard way and decided to study it instead of reporting it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_wyvern_tor.title: Wyvern Tor
ent_tpl_phandalin_wyvern_tor.subtitle: Orc camp
ent_tpl_phandalin_wyvern_tor.summary: Raider stronghold pressuring routes; can become urgent if the group ignores the frontier.
ent_tpl_phandalin_wyvern_tor.content: Wyvern Tor sits close enough to the trade roads that orc raiding parties can strike and vanish before word reaches Phandalin. Brughor tests the frontier the way a wolf tests a fence — a little further each season.

ent_tpl_phandalin_thundertree.title: Thundertree
ent_tpl_phandalin_thundertree.subtitle: Ruins of ash and growth
ent_tpl_phandalin_thundertree.summary: Ruined town where a druid, ash zombies and a young dragon compete for control.
ent_tpl_phandalin_thundertree.content: Thundertree died the night a wyrmling claimed the valley, and it never really came back. Ash zombies wander where families used to live, and Reidoth is the only one who still visits on purpose.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_town.title: Phandalin Townspeople
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_town.summary: Farmers, miners, merchants and families who want safety without provoking open war.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_town.content: Phandalin's people rebuilt fast and armed slow, trusting neighbors more than any watch that never fully materialized. They'll help anyone who helps them first — that order matters more than the size of the offer.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_cragmaw.title: Cragmaw Tribe
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_cragmaw.summary: Goblins and goblinoids divided between survival, greed and obedience to more dangerous chiefs.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_cragmaw.content: The Cragmaw tribe answers to whoever wins the last fight, which keeps loyalty short and grudges long. Klarg's hold on the hideout crew is only as solid as his last display of strength.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_rockseeker.title: Rockseeker Brothers
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_rockseeker.summary: Mine-hunters who found more than they could protect.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_rockseeker.content: Three brothers, one map and a mine that swallowed more than it gave back. What started as a family venture became a cautionary tale half of Phandalin still half-remembers.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_lords_alliance.title: Lords' Alliance
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_lords_alliance.summary: Political network interested in stabilizing Phandalin without losing prestige or resources.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_lords_alliance.content: The Alliance would rather stabilize Phandalin quietly than admit how much ground they've lost on this frontier. Sildar reports what he can, but official help is slow and comes with strings.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_zhentarim.title: Zhentarim Network
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_zhentarim.summary: Commercial contacts who see Phandalin as a future investment.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_zhentarim.content: Zhentarim agents don't need Phandalin to love them, just to owe them. A town in crisis is a town ready to sign bad contracts.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_order_gauntlet.title: Order of the Gauntlet
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_order_gauntlet.summary: Martial idealists watching for signs of corruption and abuse of power.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_order_gauntlet.content: The Order watches for the moment fear stops being incidental and starts being policy. They'd rather arrive after the Redbrands fall than intervene before — it plays better.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_emerald_enclav.title: Emerald Enclave
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_emerald_enclav.summary: Natural balance guardians worried about Thundertree and forces awakening underground.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_emerald_enclav.content: The Enclave keeps half an eye on Thundertree and the mine, worried that whatever wakes down there won't stay contained. They intervene reluctantly, and only when balance is truly at risk.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_wyvern_orcs.title: Wyvern Tor Orcs
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_wyvern_orcs.summary: Border raiders who test routes and prove their strength.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_wyvern_orcs.content: Wyvern Tor's orcs raid on their own schedule, unconnected to the Redbrands or the Black Spider. They're a second front for any DM who wants Phandalin's threats to feel bigger than one conspiracy.

ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_ash_zombies.title: Thundertree Ash Zombies
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_ash_zombies.summary: Animated remains from an ancient disaster, more symptom than conscious faction.
ent_tpl_phandalin_faction_ash_zombies.content: What's left of Thundertree doesn't plot or negotiate. It just keeps moving — a slow reminder that some dangers don't need a leader to be dangerous.

ent_tpl_phandalin_elmar.title: Elmar Barthen
ent_tpl_phandalin_elmar.subtitle: Merchant
ent_tpl_phandalin_elmar.summary: First friendly contact in town. Knows who expected Gundren and who asks uncomfortable questions.
ent_tpl_phandalin_elmar.content: Elmar's kept the books on this town's trade for a decade, which means he notices absences before anyone else does — missing caravans, missing customers, missing coin. He's cautious with strangers but generous once he decides they're not a problem.

ent_tpl_phandalin_trilena.title: Trilena Stonehill
ent_tpl_phandalin_trilena.subtitle: Town's worried voice
ent_tpl_phandalin_trilena.summary: Can put a human face on Redbrand abuses and speak what others dare not.
ent_tpl_phandalin_trilena.content: Trilena says out loud what half the town only whispers, and it's cost her more than once. She keeps talking anyway, on the theory that silence has never once made a bully go away.

ent_tpl_phandalin_harbin.title: Harbin Wester
ent_tpl_phandalin_harbin.subtitle: Nervous mayor
ent_tpl_phandalin_harbin.summary: Phandalin's official authority, more concerned with not angering the Redbrands than with leading.
ent_tpl_phandalin_harbin.content: Harbin got the mayor's chair because nobody else wanted it, and he's spent the job since then trying not to lose more than he already has. He'll sign anything that keeps the peace, even peace that isn't real.

ent_tpl_phandalin_garaele.title: Sister Garaele
ent_tpl_phandalin_garaele.subtitle: Shrine acolyte
ent_tpl_phandalin_garaele.summary: Seeks spiritual information and can guide the group toward larger threats.
ent_tpl_phandalin_garaele.content: Garaele tends the shrine more out of duty than devotion, waiting for a sign that her faith actually means something out here. She's smart enough to be dangerous to the Redbrands, if she ever decides to be.

ent_tpl_phandalin_daran.title: Daran Edermath
ent_tpl_phandalin_daran.subtitle: Retired veteran
ent_tpl_phandalin_daran.summary: Calm observer who recognizes patterns of abuse and may recommend acting against the Redbrands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_daran.content: Daran fought in wars most of Phandalin has forgotten happened, and the discipline never left him even after the uniform did. He watches the Redbrands like an old soldier watches weather, reading the signs before anyone else notices the sky changing.

ent_tpl_phandalin_carp.title: Carp Alderleaf
ent_tpl_phandalin_carp.subtitle: Too-curious child
ent_tpl_phandalin_carp.summary: Has seen more than he understands and can reveal a secret entrance if the group listens.
ent_tpl_phandalin_carp.content: Carp is twelve, curious, and convinced the adults around him are missing something obvious. He's usually right, which is either useful or a problem depending on who's listening.

ent_tpl_phandalin_mirna.title: Mirna Dendrar
ent_tpl_phandalin_mirna.subtitle: Detained neighbor
ent_tpl_phandalin_mirna.summary: Her family is the human cost of Redbrand dominion and offers an emotional, not just material, reward.
ent_tpl_phandalin_mirna.content: Mirna kept her family fed through two bad harvests and a worse winter, and she isn't about to let the Redbrands undo that. Her children are the reason she's still cooperating with people she hates.

ent_tpl_phandalin_glasstaff.title: Glasstaff
ent_tpl_phandalin_glasstaff.subtitle: Urban terror alias
ent_tpl_phandalin_glasstaff.summary: Name the Redbrands respect and the town fears. Serves as a narrative mask before Iarno is revealed.
ent_tpl_phandalin_glasstaff.content: Glasstaff is the name Iarno built to be feared, deliberately harsher and less forgiving than the man underneath it. The Redbrands answer to the mask, not the man, and that gap is exactly what makes the reveal land.

ent_tpl_phandalin_nothic.title: The Tresendar Nothic
ent_tpl_phandalin_nothic.subtitle: Hunger and secrets
ent_tpl_phandalin_nothic.summary: Creature broken by magic and mental hunger. Can be monster, informant or grotesque mirror of the place's secrets.
ent_tpl_phandalin_nothic.content: The nothic was a scholar once, before whatever happened to it in Tresendar's cellars unmade the person and left something else wearing the memory. It knows things it shouldn't, and it's hungry for more of them.

ent_tpl_phandalin_grista.title: Grista
ent_tpl_phandalin_grista.subtitle: Sleeping Giant innkeeper
ent_tpl_phandalin_grista.summary: Runs the tavern where Redbrands boast. Knows much, but prudence pays better than bravery.
ent_tpl_phandalin_grista.content: Grista's run the Sleeping Giant since before the Redbrands arrived, and she's not about to let a bad crowd take the whole business down with them. She serves drinks, keeps quiet and remembers everything.

ent_tpl_phandalin_klarg.title: Klarg
ent_tpl_phandalin_klarg.subtitle: Hideout chief
ent_tpl_phandalin_klarg.summary: Goblinoid brute who leads through force, fear and promises of bigger rewards.
ent_tpl_phandalin_klarg.content: Klarg earned his spot at the top of the hideout the old-fashioned way: by being bigger and meaner than whoever held it before him. He knows Yeemik wants his job, which makes him twice as vicious to anyone who looks like a threat.

ent_tpl_phandalin_yeemik.title: Yeemik
ent_tpl_phandalin_yeemik.subtitle: Ambitious second-in-command
ent_tpl_phandalin_yeemik.summary: Goblin willing to negotiate if it brings him closer to power in the hideout.
ent_tpl_phandalin_yeemik.content: Yeemik does the math on every situation before he commits to a side, and right now the math says Klarg is losing ground. He'll deal, betray or flee depending entirely on which option keeps him breathing.

ent_tpl_phandalin_grol.title: King Grol
ent_tpl_phandalin_grol.subtitle: Cragmaw chief
ent_tpl_phandalin_grol.summary: Goblinoid who can sell prisoners, maps or loyalties to the highest bidder.
ent_tpl_phandalin_grol.content: Grol built Cragmaw Castle's authority on fear and a willingness to sell out anyone who stops being useful. Gundren is currency to him, not a person, and he'll trade accordingly.

ent_tpl_phandalin_vyerith.title: Vyerith
ent_tpl_phandalin_vyerith.subtitle: Shapeshifter spy
ent_tpl_phandalin_vyerith.summary: Infiltrated agent who complicates Gundren's rescue and can turn certainties into suspicions.
ent_tpl_phandalin_vyerith.content: Vyerith has worn enough faces to forget which one feels most like her own, and she plays every role with the same cold precision. Her loyalty belongs entirely to whoever's paying, which right now means the Black Spider.

ent_tpl_phandalin_nundro.title: Nundro Rockseeker
ent_tpl_phandalin_nundro.subtitle: Survivor
ent_tpl_phandalin_nundro.summary: Gundren's brother. Can confirm what happened in the mine if the group finds him in time.
ent_tpl_phandalin_nundro.content: Nundro has spent longer in the dark than he'd like to admit, rationing hope the same way he rationed food. Finding him alive changes the whole tone of the mine — proof that survival here was possible.

ent_tpl_phandalin_tharden.title: Tharden Rockseeker
ent_tpl_phandalin_tharden.subtitle: Fallen brother
ent_tpl_phandalin_tharden.summary: His fate reveals the mine is no longer a promise: it is an ongoing tragedy.
ent_tpl_phandalin_tharden.content: Tharden never made it out of Wave Echo Cave, and what's left of him tells the party exactly how badly this expedition went wrong before they ever arrived. His fate is the mine's honesty, delivered without a word.

ent_tpl_phandalin_hamun.title: Hamun Kost
ent_tpl_phandalin_hamun.subtitle: Ruins mage
ent_tpl_phandalin_hamun.summary: Arcane researcher at Old Owl Well. Suspicious and dangerous, but not necessarily tied to the Black Spider.
ent_tpl_phandalin_hamun.content: Hamun came to Old Owl Well chasing a theory about the ruins' original purpose, and he's too close to an answer to leave quietly. He's not evil, exactly — just willing to overlook a lot in the name of research.

ent_tpl_phandalin_reidoth.title: Reidoth
ent_tpl_phandalin_reidoth.subtitle: Thundertree druid
ent_tpl_phandalin_reidoth.summary: Cautious guide who knows more about routes and natural dangers than almost anyone in Phandalin.
ent_tpl_phandalin_reidoth.content: Reidoth has walked every trail near Thundertree for longer than most of Phandalin has been alive, and he treats the forest's dangers with more respect than fear. He'll guide the party, but only if they listen when he says stop.

ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha.title: Agatha
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha.subtitle: Banshee of memories
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha.summary: Ancient presence who can answer questions if the group treats the encounter as an audience, not a raid.
ent_tpl_phandalin_agatha.content: Agatha remembers Conyberry as it was before the fire, and she measures every visitor against the manners of people who died centuries ago. Insult her and you get nothing; ask well and she might actually help.

ent_tpl_phandalin_brughor.title: Brughor Axe-Biter
ent_tpl_phandalin_brughor.subtitle: Raider chief
ent_tpl_phandalin_brughor.summary: Orc leader of Wyvern Tor. A direct threat to roads and farms if the group ignores the frontier.
ent_tpl_phandalin_brughor.content: Brughor leads by strength and expects to be challenged for it eventually — that's just how orc leadership works. He tests Phandalin's defenses the same way he tests his own lieutenants: by pushing until something breaks.

ent_tpl_phandalin_venomfang.title: Venomfang
ent_tpl_phandalin_venomfang.subtitle: Young cunning dragon
ent_tpl_phandalin_venomfang.summary: Thundertree's intelligent predator. His real danger lies in his ability to offer tempting deals.
ent_tpl_phandalin_venomfang.content: Venomfang is young for a dragon but old enough to know that talking gets him further than fighting most of the time. He'd rather make the party an offer they'll regret than fight a battle he might actually lose.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_escort.title: Deliver the Supplies
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_escort.subtitle: Campaign entry
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_escort.summary: Escort Gundren's wagon to Phandalin and discover why he did not arrive first.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_escort.content: This one runs almost on rails, and that's the point: give the party a clean win before the campaign gets complicated. The wagon arriving safely is Elmar's proof that the party can be trusted with bigger problems.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_dendrar.title: Rescue the Dendrar Family
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_dendrar.subtitle: Human cost of fear
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_dendrar.summary: Find Mirna and her children before the Redbrands sell, move or use them as leverage.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_dendrar.content: Every session this stays unresolved, raise the stakes a little — a rumor of a hostage moved, a guard rotation change. Rescue works best as a countdown the party feels, not just hears about.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_find_castle.title: Find Cragmaw Castle
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_find_castle.subtitle: Location search
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_find_castle.summary: Turn rumors, trail markings and testimonies into a clear route to the hidden fortress.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_find_castle.content: The castle doesn't announce itself on any map, which is deliberate. Reward investigation over travel — three good clues should matter more than three days of walking.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wave_echo.title: Reach Wave Echo Cave
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wave_echo.subtitle: Lost mine
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wave_echo.summary: Protect the map, locate the entrance and decide who controls the discovery.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wave_echo.content: This is the payoff for everything that came before it: the map, the rumors, the Rockseeker tragedy. Don't rush the reveal — let the party feel the weight of finally standing at the entrance.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_black_spider.title: Unmask the Black Spider
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_black_spider.subtitle: Antagonist arc
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_black_spider.summary: Gather enough clues to understand who is buying favors, corpses and silences across the region.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_black_spider.content: Nezznar should feel like a pattern before he feels like a person — a signature on notes, a name whispered by scared goblins, a reason things keep going wrong. Save the face-to-face for when it means something.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_lionshield.title: Recover the Lionshield Cargo
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_lionshield.subtitle: Economy at risk
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_lionshield.summary: Track stolen goods so the town sees tangible benefits from helping the group.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_lionshield.content: A smaller, self-contained win that doesn't need the main plot to matter. Good for a session that needs lower stakes after something heavier.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_agatha.title: Ask Agatha
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_agatha.subtitle: Dangerous information
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_agatha.summary: Get an answer from the banshee without turning the encounter into an absurd provocation.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_agatha.content: Agatha doesn't answer to threats, and she won't be rushed. This works best as a puzzle of courtesy and precision rather than a fight.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_old_owl.title: Investigate Old Owl Well
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_old_owl.subtitle: Arcane ruin
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_old_owl.summary: Find out why undead walk near the old well and whether Hamun Kost is threat, symptom or source.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_old_owl.content: Hamun complicates this more than the undead do. The real question isn't whether the party can win a fight — it's whether they can tell an obsessive researcher from an actual threat.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wyvern_tor.title: Drive Orcs from Wyvern Tor
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wyvern_tor.subtitle: Frontier under pressure
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wyvern_tor.summary: Resolve the raids worrying farmers and caravans.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_wyvern_tor.content: This can stay optional background pressure or escalate into a real threat, depending on how long the party ignores it. Orcs don't wait for the party to be ready.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_thundertree.title: Deal with Thundertree
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_thundertree.subtitle: Major risk
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_thundertree.summary: Find Reidoth, avoid ash zombies and decide whether approaching a young dragon is wise.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_thundertree.content: Everything here is negotiable except the ash zombies. Reidoth, Venomfang and even the ruins themselves reward a party willing to talk before it fights.

ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_future.title: Decide Phandalin's Future
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_future.subtitle: Campaign consequence
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_future.summary: After Redbrands and Cragmaw fall, decide who to strengthen: townspeople, Halia, Alliance, Rockseekers or no one.
ent_tpl_phandalin_quest_future.content: There's no clean answer here, and that's the point. Whatever the party decides, make sure it shows up in how Phandalin looks and sounds afterward.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_goblin_trail.title: Goblin trail to the stream
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_goblin_trail.subtitle: Entrance to the Cragmaw hideout
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_goblin_trail.summary: Small footprints, broken branches and a trip-rope mark a hidden path.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_goblin_trail.content: Goblins aren't careful when they think no one's watching, and this trail proves it — sloppy enough to follow, deliberate enough to be a trap if the party isn't paying attention.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_sildar_warning.title: Sildar's warning
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_sildar_warning.subtitle: Gundren knows too much
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_sildar_warning.summary: Sildar confirms Gundren found something important before disappearing.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_sildar_warning.content: Sildar doesn't know everything Gundren found, but he knows enough to be worried. His warning should feel like a nudge, not an info-dump.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redbrand_threats.title: Redbrand threats
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redbrand_threats.subtitle: Phandalin is intimidated
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redbrand_threats.summary: Several families have paid or stayed silent out of fear of Redbrand reprisals.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_redbrand_threats.content: This is less a single clue than a pattern the party has to notice — three different people, three different excuses, all pointing the same direction.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_town_fears.title: The town's fear
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_town_fears.subtitle: Nobody expects official help
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_town_fears.summary: Townspeople lower their voices when talking about the Redbrands and avoid looking at the mayor.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_town_fears.content: Nobody has to say the word "Redbrands" for this to land. The silence itself is the clue.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_carp_tunnel.title: Carp saw a secret entrance
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_carp_tunnel.subtitle: A child found what adults ignore
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_carp_tunnel.summary: Carp can describe a hidden tunnel near Tresendar if the group listens carefully.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_carp_tunnel.content: Carp saw it because nobody watches where a kid goes. That's exactly why his information is worth more than most adults' in this town.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_giant_boast.title: Sleeping Giant bravado
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_giant_boast.subtitle: The thugs talk too much
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_giant_boast.summary: A Redbrand boasts that the boss has eyes even where there are no windows.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_giant_boast.content: Drunk talk reveals more than the speaker intends, and this Redbrand has had one too many to remember he's not supposed to mention watchers.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ledger.title: Redbrand debt ledger
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ledger.subtitle: Fear turned into accounting
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ledger.summary: A record of payments, punishments and favors reveals the gang governs by economy as much as by violence.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ledger.content: The ledger turns rumor into arithmetic. Numbers don't lie about who's paying and who's being punished, even when people do.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_glasstaff_letter.title: Letter signed in blue glass
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_glasstaff_letter.subtitle: Glasstaff answers to someone
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_glasstaff_letter.summary: An incomplete message orders surveillance of adventurers asking about Gundren.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_glasstaff_letter.content: This letter proves Glasstaff isn't the top of the chain, which changes the shape of the whole investigation the moment the party reads it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nothic_whispers.title: Whispers beneath Tresendar
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nothic_whispers.subtitle: Something knows secrets it should not
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nothic_whispers.summary: An invisible presence mentions the private guilts, fears or desires of the characters.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nothic_whispers.content: The nothic doesn't lie, exactly — it just tells the truth in the cruelest possible order. What it says should feel earned, not free.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mirna_heirloom.title: Mirna's keepsake
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mirna_heirloom.subtitle: A debt of gratitude
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mirna_heirloom.summary: Mirna knows of an old family hiding spot in Thundertree and can share it if saved.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mirna_heirloom.content: Gratitude buys information that gold can't. Mirna gives this up because she trusts the party, not because she's being paid.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_halia_offer.title: Halia's offer
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_halia_offer.subtitle: Help at a political price
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_halia_offer.summary: Halia can point out Redbrand weaknesses, but wants influence in return.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_halia_offer.content: Halia never gives something for nothing, and the party should feel that math even while the offer is genuinely useful.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_harbin_notice.title: Town Master's Hall notices
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_harbin_notice.subtitle: Problems the authority outsources
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_harbin_notice.summary: The bulletin board offers rewards for external threats while avoiding any mention of the Redbrands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_harbin_notice.content: What's missing from the board says more than what's on it. Harbin will pay for wolves but not for the wolves in red cloaks standing in his own street.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_daran_report.title: Daran's old report
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_daran_report.subtitle: Patterns of occupation
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_daran_report.summary: Daran recognizes the Redbrands operate like troops under command, not loose drunks.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_daran_report.content: Daran's notes read like a soldier's field report, not a rumor. That's what makes them credible — and useful.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_linene_cargo.title: Lionshield cargo marks
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_linene_cargo.subtitle: Traceable theft
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_linene_cargo.summary: Seals on recovered crates connect goblins, trade routes and buyers who profit from chaos.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_linene_cargo.content: Trade marks don't lie about where goods came from, even when the people selling them do. This is the paper trail the party needed.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_castle_marks.title: Castle trail markers
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_castle_marks.subtitle: Incomplete Cragmaw route
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_castle_marks.summary: Goblin symbols and references to an old castle help narrow down the search area.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_castle_marks.content: Goblin symbols mean little to outsiders until someone who's fought them explains the shorthand. Even then, the route only gets clearer, not obvious.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ransom.title: Cragmaw ransom message
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ransom.subtitle: Gundren has a price
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ransom.summary: A dirty note makes clear Gundren is worth more alive than dead, at least for now.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_ransom.content: This message wasn't written by someone who cares about Gundren — it was written by someone doing math on his value. That distinction matters for how the party plans a rescue.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_vyerith_disguise.title: Details that do not fit
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_vyerith_disguise.subtitle: An identity fails under pressure
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_vyerith_disguise.summary: An apparent ally or prisoner knows too little about who they claim to be.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_vyerith_disguise.content: Good disguises fail on small details, not big ones. This is a detail small enough to miss and specific enough to matter.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_grol_bargain.title: Grol's negotiation
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_grol_bargain.subtitle: The map is merchandise
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_grol_bargain.summary: Grol is not devoted to the Black Spider; he is calculating price, fear and opportunity.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_grol_bargain.content: Grol negotiates like he's already decided to betray someone — the only question left is who gets the better price.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nundro_testimony.title: Nundro's testimony
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nundro_testimony.subtitle: The mine was already occupied
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nundro_testimony.summary: Nundro saw black spider symbols before being separated from his brothers.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_nundro_testimony.content: Nundro's account isn't dramatic, which is exactly why it's believable. Trauma rarely comes out as a clean story the first time.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_spider_sigil.title: Black spider seal
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_spider_sigil.subtitle: Antagonist's signature
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_spider_sigil.summary: A discreet seal appears on messages, payments and seemingly unconnected orders.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_spider_sigil.content: The sigil shows up everywhere once the party knows to look for it, which is its own kind of unsettling — this conspiracy has been in plain sight the whole time.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mine_map.title: Gundren's map
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mine_map.subtitle: Route to the lost mine
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mine_map.summary: The map is the object everyone wants to control.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_mine_map.content: Every faction in this campaign wants a piece of this map, which makes it worth more as leverage than as a literal route, at least until the party decides otherwise.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_forge_echo.title: Magic echo in the rock
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_forge_echo.subtitle: The forge is no legend
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_forge_echo.summary: The mine's stone holds arcane resonances that respond to spells or dwarven symbols.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_forge_echo.content: The stone remembers what it was built for, even after all this time. That resonance is proof the forge is real before anyone actually sees it work.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_tharden_body.title: Tharden's fate
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_tharden_body.subtitle: The mine already extracted a price
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_tharden_body.summary: The body or belongings of Tharden show the Black Spider arrived first and left no useful witnesses.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_tharden_body.content: Tharden's fate isn't a mystery to solve — it's a fact to absorb. Let it land before moving the party on to the next room.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_reidoth_warning.title: Reidoth's warning
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_reidoth_warning.subtitle: Not every treasure is worth the journey
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_reidoth_warning.summary: Reidoth knows routes and dangers, but warns Thundertree is no heroic stroll.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_reidoth_warning.content: Reidoth isn't trying to scare the party off, just trying to make sure they go in with open eyes. He's seen what happens to people who don't.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dragon_signs.title: Dragon signs
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dragon_signs.subtitle: The predator does not fully hide
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dragon_signs.summary: Marked trees, animal silence and acrid smells indicate something large claims Thundertree.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_dragon_signs.content: Venomfang doesn't hide well, but he doesn't need to — nothing in Thundertree is in a position to challenge him, and he knows it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_old_owl_signs.title: Old Owl Well signs
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_old_owl_signs.subtitle: Magic that does not belong here
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_old_owl_signs.summary: Ritual remains, undead and arcane notes suggest a dangerous investigation separate from the Redbrands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_old_owl_signs.content: These signs point somewhere the main plot never planned to go, which is exactly why they're worth chasing if the table has the appetite for a detour.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_agatha_memory.title: Agatha's memory
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_agatha_memory.subtitle: An answer at a cost
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_agatha_memory.summary: Agatha can remember names, maps or books, but the question must be precise.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_agatha_memory.content: Agatha's memory is precise, not generous. Ask a vague question and get a vague answer — the party has to earn specificity.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_wyvern_raids.title: Wyvern Tor raid trails
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_wyvern_raids.subtitle: The frontier is moving
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_wyvern_raids.summary: Heavy tracks and camp remains show the orcs are testing Phandalin's routes.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_wyvern_raids.content: The orcs aren't subtle, but they are patient. These tracks show a pattern, not a single incident.

ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_power_vacuum.title: Power vacuum in Phandalin
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_power_vacuum.subtitle: Victory also creates problems
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_power_vacuum.summary: When the Redbrands fall, several people try to decide what 'order' means for the town.
ent_tpl_phandalin_clue_power_vacuum.content: Winning the fight against the Redbrands doesn't end the story — it just changes who's arguing over what comes next.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar.title: The Black Spider coordinates the region
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar.subtitle: An antagonist connects goblins and bandits
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar.summary: Nezznar uses intermediaries so no one sees the full network until it is too late.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nezznar.content: Reveal this in pieces, never all at once. The scarier version of Nezznar is the one the party assembles themselves from scattered evidence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_castle.title: Cragmaw Castle hidden by false routes
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_castle.subtitle: The goblins use no single entrance
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_castle.summary: The castle cannot be found by following a main road; you must reconstruct the route.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_castle.content: The false routes aren't an accident — goblins who get lost are goblins who can't lead anyone back. Grol built confusion into the castle's defense on purpose.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_map.title: Gundren's map is the real key
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_map.subtitle: Without the map the mine remains a myth
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_map.summary: Rescuing Gundren is not enough if the map has already changed hands.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_map.content: This secret should reframe the whole quest the moment the party realizes it: finding Gundren was never the hard part.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_halia.title: Halia wants to inherit the power vacuum
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_halia.subtitle: Useful ally, not innocent
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_halia.summary: Her help against the Redbrands could create another power difficult to control.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_halia.content: Halia's help is real. So is her ambition. The party doesn't need to choose between trusting her and being wary of her — both are correct at once.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_harbin.title: Harbin knows more than he admits
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_harbin.subtitle: Fear is also a political choice
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_harbin.summary: The mayor is not ignorant of the problem: he has chosen to survive by looking away.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_harbin.content: Harbin's cowardice is deliberate, not accidental — he ran the numbers on defiance versus survival and picked survival. The party doesn't have to like it to understand it.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_cragmaw.title: The Cragmaws can be divided
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_cragmaw.subtitle: Not all obey from loyalty
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_cragmaw.summary: Yeemik, Grol and others may switch sides if they see an opportunity for advancement.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_cragmaw.content: Cragmaw loyalty is a fiction that holds only as long as Klarg looks unbeatable. Crack that image once, publicly, and the tribe starts fracturing on its own.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_forge.title: The Forge of Spells partially works
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_forge.subtitle: The central treasure is not just gold
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_forge.summary: The mine holds a damaged arcane focus that is still dangerous and valuable.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_forge.content: Nobody in this campaign fully understands what the forge can still do, including Nezznar. That uncertainty is worth more dramatic tension than a fixed answer would be.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_dendrars.title: The Dendrar family is still alive
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_dendrars.subtitle: Victims on a countdown
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_dendrars.summary: The Redbrands keep hostages who may disappear if the group delays Tresendar too long.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_dendrars.content: The hostages are leverage until the moment the Redbrands decide they're a liability instead. That moment should arrive on the DM's schedule, not the dice's.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nothic.title: The nothic knows Tresendar's secrets
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nothic.subtitle: A useful monster if you pay the price
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nothic.summary: The creature beneath the manor knows who commands, who lies and what intruders fear.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_nothic.content: The nothic's information isn't free — it costs something small from whoever asks, a memory or a truth they'd rather not share aloud. Fair trade, for a monster.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hostages.title: The Redbrands plan to move the hostages
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hostages.subtitle: Delay has a cost
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hostages.summary: If the group does not act, hostages may be moved out of Phandalin or used as insurance.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hostages.content: This clock should be felt, not announced. A guard mentioning it in passing does more work than a countdown on the table.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_vyerith.title: Vyerith is not who she seems
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_vyerith.subtitle: The castle holds a second lie
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_vyerith.summary: A shapeshifter agent can steal the map even if the group wins the main fight.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_vyerith.content: Vyerith isn't the point where the mystery ends — she's the twist that keeps it going a little longer, right when the party thinks they've won.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_tharden.title: Tharden is already dead in the mine
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_tharden.subtitle: Victory comes too late for someone
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_tharden.summary: Nundro's rescue does not repair the full Rockseeker tragedy.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_tharden.content: There's no rescue here, only closure. Let Nundro's grief do the emotional work instead of a fight scene.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hamun.title: Hamun Kost is not the central villain
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hamun.subtitle: Lateral threat, not root cause
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hamun.summary: His necromancy is dangerous, but not every strange thread belongs to the Black Spider.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_hamun.content: Hamun is a distraction the party can choose to engage with or walk past. Either choice should feel valid.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_venomfang.title: Venomfang prefers manipulation over combat
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_venomfang.subtitle: A dragon also negotiates
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_venomfang.summary: The Thundertree dragon can offer routes, lies or favors to use the group against his own problems.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_venomfang.content: A fight with Venomfang the party can't win outright is more memorable than one they can — as long as the alternative he offers feels genuinely tempting.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_agatha.title: Agatha knows more than one answer
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_agatha.subtitle: The question matters
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_agatha.summary: The banshee can reveal a location, name or memory, but will not solve the whole campaign for free.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_agatha.content: Agatha rewards precision over persistence. The party that asks three careful questions gets more than the party that asks ten desperate ones.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_wyvern.title: The orcs test Phandalin's weakness
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_wyvern.subtitle: The frontier reads fear
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_wyvern.summary: If the Redbrands dominate the town, other threats understand no one protects the roads.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_wyvern.content: This secret only matters if the party lets the Redbrands stand long enough for word to spread. It's a consequence waiting on another consequence.

ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_power_vacuum.title: Freeing Phandalin opens a political struggle
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_power_vacuum.subtitle: The urban ending is not neutral
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_power_vacuum.summary: When the Redbrands fall, Halia, Harbin, Sildar, Daran and townspeople compete to define the new order.
ent_tpl_phandalin_secret_power_vacuum.content: There's no version of this ending where everyone's happy. Pick the two or three loudest voices and let their conflict carry the epilogue.

rel_tpl_phandalin_trail_hideout.description: The tracks lead from the road to the goblin hideout.

rel_tpl_phandalin_gundren_quest.description: Finding Gundren keeps the campaign moving.

rel_tpl_phandalin_hideout_gundren.description: The hideout reveals that Gundren was moved.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_town.description: The town avoids acting because it fears retaliation.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_tresendar.description: The base of operations lies beneath Tresendar Manor.

rel_tpl_phandalin_iarno_redbrands.description: Glasstaff leads the Redbrands from the shadows.

rel_tpl_phandalin_iarno_secret.description: His double identity should be revealed through clues, not free exposition.

rel_tpl_phandalin_tresendar_secret.description: Exploring Tresendar can reveal correspondence or proof.

rel_tpl_phandalin_nezznar_wave.description: Nezznar understands the mine’s true value.

rel_tpl_phandalin_wave_gundren.description: The final location depends on Gundren’s map.

rel_tpl_phandalin_redbrands_consequence.description: Resolving the urban arc transforms Phandalin’s behavior.

rel_tpl_phandalin_inn_toblen.description: The inn lets the party hear the town’s fear through Toblen and his customers.

rel_tpl_phandalin_toblen_redbrands.description: Toblen stays quiet because the Redbrands can punish his family.

rel_tpl_phandalin_halia_redbrands.description: Halia wants the Redbrands gone, but not out of altruism.

rel_tpl_phandalin_qelline_hideout.description: Qelline knows people who can point toward less-watched routes.

rel_tpl_phandalin_clay_tresendar.description: The red clay leads toward the path beneath Tresendar.

rel_tpl_phandalin_blackseal_nezznar.description: The black seal suggests someone outside town is directing the conflict.

rel_tpl_phandalin_map_wave.description: The torn map makes the mine a visible promise without revealing the whole route.

rel_tpl_phandalin_break_fear_consequence.description: Breaking the fear lets Phandalin start acting as a community.

rel_tpl_phandalin_street_breakfear.description: The main-street fight can turn a complaint into a concrete quest.

rel_tpl_phandalin_nezznar_secret.description: Nezznar’s influence appears first as money, notes and indirect orders.

rel_tpl_phandalin_shrine_rumor.description: The shrine gathers rumors neighbors will not say in the inn.

rel_tpl_phandalin_linene_trail.description: Linene needs safe roads to keep frontier trade alive.

fact_tpl_phandalin_1.statement: Gundren disappeared before the supplies reached Phandalin.

fact_tpl_phandalin_2.statement: The Redbrands control the town’s fear more than its physical territory.

fact_tpl_phandalin_3.statement: Iarno and Glasstaff are the same person.

fact_tpl_phandalin_4.statement: Wave Echo Cave is not only a mine: it contains a coveted source of magical power.

fact_tpl_phandalin_5.statement: If the party saves Phandalin from the Redbrands, neighbors will start offering information without interrogation.

fact_tpl_phandalin_6.statement: Toblen knows which neighbors pay the Redbrands, but needs to see that the party can protect specific people.

fact_tpl_phandalin_7.statement: Halia can be a temporary ally against the Redbrands, though she will try to fill the power vacuum they leave.

fact_tpl_phandalin_8.statement: The black-seal note proves Glasstaff is under pressure from someone interested in the mine.

fact_tpl_phandalin_9.statement: Gundren’s map works best if handed out incomplete: the table gets a clear promise without skipping the town arc.

fact_tpl_phandalin_10.statement: If the characters resolve a public threat without humiliating the town, neighbors will begin offering voluntary help.

fact_tpl_phandalin_11.statement: Qelline has no political power, but she does have a trusted network that avoids unnecessary fights.

fact_tpl_phandalin_12.statement: Nezznar prefers adventurers wasting time on local trouble before they look toward Wave Echo Cave.

fact_tpl_phandalin_13.statement: Gundren's map works as a campaign clock: every delay increases the chance it changes hands.

fact_tpl_phandalin_14.statement: Vyerith can turn a victory at Cragmaw Castle into a chase if she steals or replaces key information.

fact_tpl_phandalin_15.statement: Tharden Rockseeker has already paid the price of Wave Echo, preventing the ending from being just a race for loot.

fact_tpl_phandalin_16.statement: Wave Echo Cave holds a real magical source: deteriorated but still powerful.

fact_tpl_phandalin_17.statement: Hamun Kost is a lateral threat or an uncomfortable source, not an automatic extension of the Black Spider.

fact_tpl_phandalin_18.statement: Venomfang should appear as a negotiating, intelligent danger; the table should understand that not every challenge is meant to be won by force.

fact_tpl_phandalin_19.statement: Defeating the Redbrands and the Black Spider raises a political question: who protects Phandalin when the adventurers leave?

fact_tpl_phandalin_20.statement: The next prepared session should open with a clear decision: enter Tresendar, seek more local support or pursue the Cragmaw trail to the castle.

prep.summary: Direct opening: attack, trail, rescue and first emotional link to Gundren’s disappearance.
prep.openingPrompt: The road narrows between trees. Two horses block the path. They do not neigh. They do not move. And the arrows are already ready.
goals: Reveal that Gundren is in danger.
Offer a clear decision: follow the trail or protect the cargo.

chk_ph_1.label: Show the horses before the attack.

chk_ph_2.label: Give the group a visible clue toward the hideout.

prep.summary: Present Phandalin as a living place and prepare the Redbrand arc.
prep.openingPrompt: Phandalin looks quiet until someone lowers their voice when a red cloak appears across the street.
goals: Show the Redbrands as a nearby problem.

chk_ph_3.label: Make the town’s fear visible, not explained.

prep.summary: Resolve the Redbrand arc through infiltration, combat, rescue or negotiation.
prep.openingPrompt: The manor does not look like a fortress. That is the problem: nobody wants to look underneath.
goals: Reveal Glasstaff clues if players investigate.

chk_ph_4.label: Close by asking what changes in Phandalin after the victory.

sess_tpl_phandalin_allies.title: Session 4 — Allies in an awakening town

prep.summary: After striking the Redbrands, the group can turn scattered favors into real town support.
prep.openingPrompt: The next morning, no one looks at the adventurers the same way. Some lower their voices; others, for the first time, approach.
goals: Show consequences of protecting or ignoring specific neighbors.
Deliver a clue toward the larger network behind Glasstaff.

ph_s4_pnjs.label: Choose three NPCs who react to what happened.

ph_s4_public.label: Decide what information is now public for the player portal.

ph_s4_next.label: Close with a clue toward Tresendar or the mine.

sess_tpl_phandalin_wave.title: Session 5 — Echoes of the lost mine

prep.summary: The campaign points toward Wave Echo Cave: promise of treasure, rescue and truth about the Black Spider.
prep.openingPrompt: The map fragment finally matches Gundren’s marks. The route leads not only to a mine, but to an old promise everyone wants to claim.
goals: Connect the local arc to the larger threat.
Prepare the next stretch if the table wants to continue.

ph_s5_map.label: Decide which part of the map is still missing.

ph_s5_nezznar.label: Show one indirect action by Nezznar before revealing him.

ph_s5_continue.label: Prepare a choice: rescue, negotiate or pursue.

prep.summary: Transition and investigation session: turn loose clues into a route, and decide whether to pursue Gundren directly or clear secondary fronts first.

prep_castle_sources.label: Prepare two route sources: goblin, Reidoth, document or marks

prep_grol_deal.label: Define what Grol wants in a negotiation

prep_vyerith_tells.label: Note three details that betray Vyerith without making it obvious

prep.summary: Campaign climax: confront Nezznar and decide what happens to the Forge of Spells.

prep_nezznar_offer.label: Prepare a tempting offer from Nezznar

prep_forge_choices.label: Define 3 options for the Forge: protect, exploit, seal

prep_final_consequence.label: Prepare a different consequence depending on who controls the mine

prep.summary: Short closing session to resolve social decisions, distribute rewards and plant seeds for continuation.

prep_epilogue_questions.label: Prepare a legacy question for each character

prep_power_outcome.label: Choose how Phandalin changes based on favored allies

prep_next_seed.label: Leave one optional hook for a future campaign

note_tpl_ph_family.title: Family table
note_tpl_ph_family.text: Make decisions visible: help the town, search for Gundren or investigate the manor. Do not hide the fun behind too many names.

cvs_tpl_phandalin_investigation.description: Clue trail from first discovery to hidden secrets.

cvs_tpl_phandalin_locations.description: All key locations and travel routes across the region.
